body_txt: This is a phenomenon common to superhero stories, in which whenever villains (or just obnoxious people who'd be dangerous to the hero if they knew these things) discover the hero's secret identity (or hidden base or other important secret), then the viewer can expect that they won't be long for the world. The violator finds out the hard way that Status Quo Is God and is promptly killed or worse in that same story/arc/episode/whatever before they can blow the hero's cover by spreading the information around. This is done to heighten the dramatic tension, as the hero and enemies are on a level playing field with the villains' threat level elevated. However, the writers (usually) don't let characters in the know last that long, since if there's a bad guy out there who knows the biggest secret of the mythos, he'll most certainly eclipse all other bad guys and never cease to be the most serious threat until he's put down for good. Non-lethal alternatives that prevent these potential troublemakers from exploiting the secret are also fairly common, e.g. a coma, amnesia (total or merely laser-guided ), mental breakdown, or imprisonment (particularly if the latter renders them unable to communicate ). A Sub-Trope of It's Personal and Killed to Uphold the Masquerade , but in this situation it's normally the writers' decision to pull the Secret Identity trigger when they need to raise the stakes on a particular story/arc/series, but don't want to deal with long-term consequences.
